mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive 20-day returns dominate in >60% of months when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden jump in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and rising input costs, compressing profit margins and causing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real rates turn negative, eroding purchasing power and prompting equity market sell-offs over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative real rates correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns in >65% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumption capacity, driving corporate earnings growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10) over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP signals economic recession, triggering immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities and negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >70% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling unemployment indicates productive capacity expansion without labor market overheating, supporting equity valuations over one month.
failure_condition: The INDPRO/UNRATE divergence pattern yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of occurrences.
